implementation_approach: We will use React for the frontend to create a responsive and interactive user interface. For real-time communication, we will use WebSocket with Socket.IO to ensure low-latency game state synchronization. The backend will be built using Node.js with Express for handling game logic and user authentication. MongoDB will be used for storing user profiles and game statistics. Tailwind CSS will be used for styling to ensure a clean and modern design.
data_structures_and_interfaces:
classDiagram
    class GameRoom {
        <<entity>>
        +room_id: str
        +players: List[Player]
        +game_state: GameState
        +add_player(player: Player)
        +remove_player(player_id: str)
        +update_game_state(new_state: GameState)
    }
    class Player {
        <<entity>>
        +player_id: str
        +username: str
        +hand: List[Card]
        +play_card(card: Card)
    }
    class GameState {
        <<entity>>
        +current_turn: str
        +board: List[Card]
        +update_turn(player_id: str)
    }
    class Card {
        <<entity>>
        +card_id: str
        +type: str
        +value: int
    }
    GameRoom --> Player
    GameRoom --> GameState
    Player --> Card
program_call_flow:
sequenceDiagram
    participant P as Player
    participant GR as GameRoom
    participant GS as GameState
    P->>GR: join_game(room_id)
    GR->>GS: initialize_game_state()
    GS-->>GR: game_state
    GR-->>P: game_state
    P->>GR: play_card(card)
    GR->>GS: update_game_state(card)
    GS-->>GR: updated_game_state
    GR-->>P: updated_game_state
anything_unclear: Clarification needed on the specific game mechanics and rules of 'Bunny Warren' to ensure accurate implementation of the game logic.
